findings.title: Exported class ActivityMonitor lacks class-level JSDoc
findings.description: ActivityMonitor is an exported public class (re-exported from index.ts) but has no class-level JSDoc. All other exported interfaces in this codebase (ActivityEvent, ActivityContext, ActivityState, ProcessedContext, etc.) have interface-level JSDoc. This class is the primary consumer-facing component of the activity monitoring subsystem and would benefit from a summary describing its role, lifecycle (start/stop), and relationship to ProcessedContext output.
findings.suggestion:
Add a class-level JSDoc comment, e.g.:
/**
 * Monitors user activity events and periodically aggregates them into ProcessedContext snapshots.
 * Call recordEvent() to feed raw events, start()/stop() to control periodic aggregation,
 * and tick() to manually trigger a snapshot.
 */

findings.title: Public method tick() has non-obvious side effects undocumented
findings.description: The tick() method has two non-obvious side effects that are not documented: (1) it clears the screenshot context after building the ProcessedContext, meaning screenshot data is only included in a single tick; (2) it invokes the onContext callback and swallows errors into onError. A future maintainer calling tick() may not expect either behavior without reading the implementation. The other public methods (recordEvent, setScreenshotContext, start, stop) are sufficiently self-documenting, but tick() is not.
findings.suggestion:
Add JSDoc to tick():
/**
 * Produce a ProcessedContext snapshot from current state.
 * Clears the screenshot context after use (consumed once per tick).
 * Invokes onContext callback; errors are forwarded to onError.
 */

findings.title: VlmProvider JSDoc references version-specific SDK name 'AI SDK 6'
findings.code_snippet:
/**
 * Interface for Vision Language Model providers.
 * Implementations wrap specific LLM SDKs (e.g., AI SDK 6, xsAI).
 */
findings.description: The comment references 'AI SDK 6' as a specific example SDK. Version-specific references in interface documentation rot quickly -- when a new version ships or the SDK is replaced, this comment becomes misleading. The 'xsAI' reference is less problematic since it is the project's actual LLM SDK dependency.
findings.suggestion: Remove the version number or generalize: 'Implementations wrap specific LLM SDKs (e.g., xsAI, Vercel AI SDK).'

positive_observations: All exported interfaces in types.ts have thorough JSDoc with field-level documentation -- consistent with project conventions
ActivityMonitorOptions has accurate default values documented in JSDoc that match the corresponding constants
No forbidden patterns (TODO/FIXME/HACK/PLACEHOLDER/TEMP) found in any changed file
Test file uses clear descriptive test names that serve as living documentation of expected behavior
Error wrapping in tick() uses the cause chain pattern (new Error(msg, { cause })) which is well-documented by the error message
